enonce.texte:
Read the following text and identify the Past Simple and Past Continuous verbs.

Last weekend, I was visiting my grandmother when I received a strange phone call. While I was talking to her, my phone suddenly rang. I answered it and heard a voice I didn't recognize. The person asked for my father. I explained that he wasn't at home. Then I hung up and continued my conversation with my grandmother. Later, I discovered that it was a wrong number.

questions.texte: List all the verbs in the Past Simple tense.
questions.reponse: received, answered, heard, asked, explained, hung up, discovered, was (was isn't a past simple verb, it's past simple of 'be' but it's a state verb, however in this context it is used as a past simple verb). The correct list is: received, answered, heard, asked, explained, hung up, discovered.

questions.texte: List all the verbs in the Past Continuous tense.
questions.reponse: was visiting, was talking

questions.texte: Identify one sentence where the Past Continuous is interrupted by the Past Simple.
questions.reponse: I was visiting my grandmother when I received a strange phone call. OR While I was talking to her, my phone suddenly rang.

questions.texte: Explain the difference in use between the two tenses in this text.
questions.reponse: The Past Simple is used for completed actions in the past (e.g., I received a call, I answered it). The Past Continuous is used to describe an action that was in progress in the past (e.g., I was visiting my grandmother, I was talking to her). The Past Simple often interrupts a longer action in the Past Continuous.

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct form of the verb in brackets (Past Continuous).

questions.texte: I __________ (study) when the lights went out.
questions.reponse: was studying

questions.texte: They __________ (play) football when it started to rain.
questions.reponse: were playing

questions.texte: She __________ (not / sleep) when he came back.
questions.reponse: was not sleeping / wasn't sleeping

questions.texte: We __________ (watch) a documentary about the environment at 8 PM.
questions.reponse: were watching

questions.texte: __________ you __________ (listen) to the news when the announcement was made?
questions.reponse: Were you listening

enonce.texte: Combine the following ideas using 'when' or 'while' to create sentences.

questions.texte: I was reading a book. My friend called me.
questions.reponse: I was reading a book when my friend called me. OR While I was reading a book, my friend called me.

questions.texte: The students were taking an exam. The fire alarm rang.
questions.reponse: The students were taking an exam when the fire alarm rang. OR While the students were taking an exam, the fire alarm rang.

questions.texte: My mother was cooking dinner. My father came home.
questions.reponse: My mother was cooking dinner when my father came home. OR While my mother was cooking dinner, my father came home.

questions.texte: The team was training hard. The coach announced the match date.
questions.reponse: The team was training hard when the coach announced the match date. OR While the team was training hard, the coach announced the match date.

enonce.texte:
Read the text about a protest and answer the questions.

Yesterday morning, while many people were going to work, a group of students began a peaceful protest near the main roundabout. They were demanding better conditions at their school. A police officer approached the group while they were chanting slogans. He listened to their concerns and promised to talk to the authorities. While he was speaking to the leaders, some of the students started to get restless. The officer decided to call for backup. Fortunately, the situation remained calm, and the protest ended peacefully an hour later.

questions.texte: Analyze the use of the Past Simple and Past Continuous in the text. Why did the author use these tenses?
questions.reponse: The author uses the Past Simple to describe the main events that happened and the Past Continuous to set the scene and describe actions that were in progress when something else happened. For example, 'were going' describes the ongoing action of people going to work, while 'began' is the main action. This creates a clear chronology.

questions.texte: What was the students' purpose for the protest? Was there any violence?
questions.reponse: The students were demanding better conditions at their school. There was no violence, the situation remained calm.

questions.texte: Interpret the officer's reaction. Was he helpful? Why or why not?
questions.reponse: Yes, the officer was helpful. He approached the students, listened to them, and promised to talk to the authorities. This shows he wanted to resolve the problem peacefully.

questions.texte: Imagine you were one of the protesting students. Write 3 sentences describing what you were doing during the protest and what happened.
questions.reponse: Example: I was holding a sign and shouting slogans when the police officer arrived. While he was talking to our leaders, I was feeling very anxious. When the protest ended, I felt relieved.

enonce.texte:
Write a short story (about 150-200 words) about a memorable event in your life. Use both the Past Simple and Past Continuous correctly. Choose one of the following topics:

  • The day I met a famous person.
  • A cultural ceremony I attended (e.g., a wedding, a funeral, a village festival).
  • A day everything went wrong.
  • An experience with a traditional healer.

questions.texte: Write your story here. Make sure to use at least 5 examples of Past Simple and 5 examples of Past Continuous.
questions.reponse: Example answer: It was a rainy evening while I was walking home from school. Suddenly, I saw an old woman standing under a mango tree. She was wearing a colorful traditional dress. I was curious, so I approached her. While I was talking to her, she told me she was a traditional healer. She was collecting medicinal plants. I asked her if she could help my sick grandmother. She agreed and gave me some herbs. While she was explaining how to use the herbs, I felt a deep sense of peace. Then, the rain stopped, and I thanked her. I ran home and prepared the medicine. Now, I still remember that meeting vividly.

enonce.texte: Find and correct the mistakes in the following sentences. All sentences contain a tense error.

questions.texte: I was seeing him yesterday at the market.
questions.reponse: I saw him yesterday at the market. (The action is completed, so Past Simple is correct. 'Was seeing' is used for actions in progress.)

questions.texte: While they had dinner, the lights went out.
questions.reponse: While they were having dinner, the lights went out. (Use Past Continuous for the action in progress.)

questions.texte: She was coming home and found a lost child.
questions.reponse: She came home and found a lost child. (Use Past Simple for a series of completed actions.) OR She was coming home when she found a lost child.

questions.texte: The students were not understanding the lesson, so the teacher repeated it.
questions.reponse: The students did not understand the lesson, so the teacher repeated it. ('Understand' is a stative verb, not usually used in continuous forms.)
